pair_convention.spot_explanation: EUR/JPY at 184.65 means 1 EUR buys 184.65 JPY. A rising EUR/JPY rate means the Euro is strengthening against the Japanese Yen (each Euro buys more Yen). A falling EUR/JPY rate means the Euro is weakening against the Japanese Yen (each Euro buys fewer Yen, i.e., the Yen is strengthening).
pair_convention.bearish_means: pair FALLS (EUR weakens vs JPY, i.e., JPY strengthens)

executive_summary: EUR/JPY trades at 184.65 on 9 May 2026, positioned in the 90th percentile of its 52-week range (155.59–187.96) after rallying approximately 18.7% from the March 2025 lows. Over the past month the pair has declined 1.05%, reflecting the sharp drop from the 187.96 high on 17 April to the 182.38 low on 30 April — driven by the BOJ's unprecedented 6-3 hawkish vote and suspected MoF intervention — before recovering to current levels. The dominant bilateral narrative is a tension between ECB hawkishness (81% probability of a June +25bp hike) and BOJ hawkishness (three dissenters calling for an immediate hike to 1.00%), creating a scenario where both central banks are tightening but from vastly different starting points. The +125bp ECB-BOJ rate differential structurally supports EUR/JPY, but that differential is at risk of narrowing if the BOJ delivers a June surprise and the ECB's hike path is constrained by collapsing services PMI at 47.4. The pair's extreme PPP overvaluation of approximately 33% above fair value creates substantial mean-reversion pressure at the strategic horizon, even as carry dynamics continue to provide a floor. Headline stance: Mildly Bullish EUR/JPY at the tactical horizon (Neutral, conviction 2/5) and Bearish at the strategic horizon (conviction 2/5), reflecting genuine cross-currents from dual hawkish central banks, the pair's extreme PPP overvaluation, and heightened event risk from both June meetings falling within days of each other (ECB 4-5 June, BOJ 16-17 June).

market_context.valuation_assessment: EUR/JPY at 184.65 is approximately 33% above OECD PPP fair value of ~138 (from the EUR dossier) and ~110 (from the JPY dossier). This is among the most extreme PPP overvaluations in the G10 cross space. The BIS REER for JPY sits in the bottom 5-10th percentile of post-1990 history, while EUR REER is modestly above its long-run average. The pair's current level is consistent with structurally wide rate differentials and compressed JPY safe-haven premiums during the energy shock — but strategic mean-reversion pressure is substantial.

positioning.net_pair_implication: The combined positioning implication for EUR/JPY is mildly bearish: specs are not net long EUR aggressively (15th percentile), and they are actively long JPY (75th percentile), creating a positioning headwind for the pair. Asset managers are structurally long EUR (+290,898) and short JPY (-26,550), which provides slow-burn support. The EUR/JPY carry trade community remains engaged but less aggressively than 2024, with compressed carry-to-vol ratios reducing the incentive for fresh long EUR/JPY positions.
positioning.crowding_assessment: Net neutral-to-short EUR/JPY in speculative community; carry trade not crowded but also not rebuilding

pair_technicals.key_moving_averages: 50d SMA: 185.32 (spot below), 100d SMA: 184.32 (spot just above), 200d SMA: 182.66 (spot above). MAs are not cleanly stacked — 50d is above 100d and 200d, but spot has slipped below the 50d, suggesting the near-term uptrend is under pressure.
pair_technicals.chart_pattern: Post-shock consolidation after sharp reversal from 187.96 high; spot testing 100d SMA support at 184.32 after bouncing from 182.38 low; 185 area is the key tactical battle zone — a close above 185.82 (61.8% Fibonacci) would confirm uptrend resumption. Bigger picture uptrend from 114.42 (2020 low) targets 194.88 medium-term projection.

pair_volatility.rv_vs_iv: Realised volatility (~11-13%) slightly exceeds implied volatility (~10-11%), suggesting directional spot positioning is being rewarded over options. The variance risk premium is negative (RV > IV), favouring spot expression.
pair_volatility.risk_reversals: Mild bearish skew for EUR/JPY inferred from JPY-call premium (USD/JPY put bid) post-intervention on the JPY side; magnitude moderate.
pair_volatility.vol_term_structure: Mild backwardation through the June ECB-BOJ event cluster, reflecting event premium in near-term expiries over the June 4-17 dual meeting window.

market_context.recent_regime: EUR/JPY rallied aggressively from mid-March through mid-April, gaining approximately 6 big figures from the 182 area to the 187.96 high as the ECB hawkish pivot and risk-on sentiment supported the pair. The 28 April BOJ 6-3 vote and subsequent 1 May MoF intervention on USD/JPY triggered a sharp reversal, with EUR/JPY dropping from 187.32 to 183.76 on 30 April before recovering to the 184.65 area. The pair is now in a post-shock consolidation phase, digesting the competing signals from both central bank pivots. The dominant driver has shifted from unidirectional carry optimism to a two-way rate-convergence narrative where both central banks are hawkish.

bilateral_carry_analysis.breakeven_move: Spot can move approximately 192 pips per month (annualised carry of 125bp × 184.65 ÷ 12) before carry is erased at 1M; at 3M breakeven is ~570 pips; at 6M ~1,150 pips — easily eclipsed by a single intervention day.

forward_points.1m: -10 pips (EUR at modest discount, reflecting +125bp carry advantage to EUR)

bilateral_carry_analysis.carry_assessment: The ECB deposit rate at 2.00% versus the BOJ policy rate at 0.75% creates a +125bp annualised carry in favour of EUR/JPY longs. With 1M IV estimated at ~10.5%, the carry-to-vol ratio is approximately 0.12 — well below the 0.30+ threshold at which carry trades historically work reliably. The carry regime is structurally positive for EUR/JPY longs but the carry-to-vol ratio is unattractive and declining as both central banks tighten from different directions. If the ECB delivers one hike (+25bp to 2.25%) and the BOJ delivers one hike (+25bp to 1.00%), the carry remains at +125bp (unchanged); but if the BOJ delivers two hikes before the ECB delivers its second, carry compresses to +100bp. The trajectory of the differential — not the level — is the key signal. The forward curve barely prices any EUR/JPY depreciation (reflecting the carry advantage), while our fundamental view targets 200-400 pips below forwards, representing capturable edge via short EUR/JPY spot or EUR/JPY put options.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The ECB-BOJ rate differential of +125bp (2.00% vs 0.75%) favours EUR/JPY longs, but both central banks are pivoting hawkish simultaneously, creating a partially offsetting dynamic. The ECB has ~81% probability of a June +25bp hike priced, while the BOJ has ~35-40% probability of a June hike to 1.00%. If both deliver, the differential remains at +125bp; if neither delivers, it also stays at +125bp. The asymmetric tactical scenario is a BOJ surprise hike without ECB follow-through (differential compresses to +100bp, EUR/JPY drops 200-400 pips) or ECB hike without BOJ follow-through (differential widens to +150bp, EUR/JPY rallies 100-200 pips). The 10Y Bund-JGB spread at approximately +56bp (Bund 3.07% vs JGB 2.50%) is narrow and has been compressing as JGB yields hit 1997 highs, reducing the long-end carry tailwind that supported EUR/JPY through 2024-25. The front-end differential trajectory is the critical variable: OIS-implied ECB year-end at ~2.99% versus OIS-implied BOJ path of ~0.85-1.00% suggests the ECB hike path is priced to outpace the BOJ path, which is mildly EUR/JPY-positive on the margin.
key_bilateral_metrics: ECB deposit rate: 2.00% vs BOJ policy rate: 0.75% = +125bp differential
10Y Bund-JGB spread: +56bp (narrowest in years, compressing)
ECB June hike probability: ~81% vs BOJ June hike probability: ~35-40%
Annualised carry for EUR/JPY longs: +125bp; carry-to-vol ratio: ~0.12
Breakeven move (1M): ~192 pips — easily overwhelmed by event risk
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price the ECB to hike more aggressively than the BOJ over the next 6 months (~99bp ECB vs ~25-50bp BOJ), keeping the differential broadly stable or mildly widening. The tactical mispricing is in the BOJ June probability — we see ~45-50% given the three-way dissent, higher than market-implied ~35-40%.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 6 months, the rate-differential trajectory favours EUR/JPY downside. The BOJ is on a structural normalisation path with FY2026 core CPI revised to 2.8% and three Board members already voting for 1.00%. The ECB's hike path is constrained by stagflation — services PMI at 47.4 creates a credible ceiling on cumulative tightening. If the BOJ delivers two hikes (to 1.25%) and the ECB delivers two hikes (to 2.50%), the differential compresses from +125bp to +125bp — unchanged. But the BOJ's direction of change is accelerating while the ECB's is decelerating given growth weakness. The 10Y spread compression from ~56bp toward 30-40bp by year-end would remove a key EUR/JPY support pillar. The strategic carry framework shows the carry-to-vol ratio declining further as both central banks raise rates but JPY vol stays elevated on intervention risk.
key_bilateral_metrics: Projected ECB rate end-2026: ~2.50-3.00% vs projected BOJ rate: 1.00-1.25%
Projected differential compression: from +125bp to +100-125bp (modest)
Projected 10Y Bund-JGB spread: 30-40bp (from 56bp currently)
BOJ hike velocity vs ECB: accelerating vs decelerating
Forward-implied EUR/JPY depreciation: ~1.5% annualised from forward points
strategic.what_is_priced: Aggregated bank research forecasts EUR/JPY at 184.25 by mid-2026, 182.27 by September 2026, and 180.48 by December 2026. This implies ~4 big figures of downside by year-end — broadly consistent with our view but potentially too conservative if the BOJ accelerates.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical neutral (60) reflects the near-term balance of dual hawkish pivots with the ECB slightly better priced to deliver. Strategic bearish (60) reflects the trajectory of rate convergence favouring JPY over 6 months as the BOJ normalisation path is more elastic than the ECB's growth-constrained path.

carry_analysis.trajectory: Differential stable at +125bp if both central banks deliver one hike each; compresses to +100bp if BOJ delivers two hikes before ECB second hike. Trajectory of differential — not level — is the key signal.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The eurozone-Japan growth differential is narrowing to the point of indistinguishability: eurozone Q1 GDP at +0.1% QoQ versus Japan's Q4 2025 GDP at +0.3% QoQ. The eurozone's growth collapse is more acute — services PMI at 47.4 (contraction) versus Japan's composite PMI at 52.4 (expansion) — creating a growth-differential signal that favours JPY. The inflation differential is marginal: eurozone HICP at 3.0% versus Japan national core CPI at 1.8%, but the composition differs fundamentally. Eurozone inflation is energy-driven with softening core (HICPX 2.2%), while Japan's inflation is wage-driven with sticky core-core (2.4%). The wage channel (Shunto 5.09%) provides the BOJ more policy room to hike than the ECB's energy-driven impulse provides Lagarde. Data surprise dynamics favour JPY: Japanese data is surprising mildly to the upside (Tankan, retail sales) while eurozone data is collapsing (IFO at COVID lows, services PMI multi-year lows). This creates an asymmetric sensitivity where the next eurozone services PMI print has a larger EUR/JPY impact than the next Japanese data release.
key_bilateral_metrics: EZ Q1 GDP: +0.1% QoQ vs Japan Q4 GDP: +0.3% QoQ
EZ services PMI: 47.4 vs Japan composite PMI: 52.4
EZ HICP: 3.0% vs Japan core CPI: 1.8%
Shunto 2026 wage hike: 5.09% — no eurozone equivalent structural driver
EZ IFO: 84.4 (COVID lows) vs Japan Tankan large mfg DI: +17 (solid)
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price eurozone stagflation but weight the ECB rate response over the growth shock. The mispricing: if the May services PMI breaks below 46, ECB hike probability drops sharply, removing EUR/JPY's primary support.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 6 months, the growth differential is more nuanced. Japan's FY2026 GDP projection of +0.5% is below the eurozone's +0.9%, but Japan has structural fiscal stimulus (Takaichi ¥21.3T supplementary + ¥120T budget) while the eurozone has German infrastructure stimulus partially offset by French political instability. The inflation persistence channel favours JPY: Japan's wage-price cycle (locked in by Shunto) provides durable inflation support that enables sustained BOJ normalisation, while eurozone inflation may fade rapidly if energy prices normalise. The IMF Article IV assessment projects Japan's growth remaining strong in 2026 with an above-potential output gap. Japan's fiscal impulse and structural wage inflation create a more durable backdrop for BOJ normalisation than the ECB's energy-driven tightening cycle that is already straining against a services recession.
key_bilateral_metrics: BOJ FY2026 GDP: 0.5% vs ECB Staff eurozone GDP: 0.9%
BOJ FY2026 core CPI: 2.8% vs ECB Staff HICP: 2.6%
Japan Shunto wage persistence: 5.09% (structural) vs EZ wage tracker: easing
Japan fiscal stimulus: ¥21.3T supplementary + ¥120T budget
Eurozone fiscal: German €500bn infrastructure (positive) offset by French fiscal stress (negative)
strategic.what_is_priced: Strategic markets price moderate growth divergence favouring the eurozone. We disagree: Japan's wage-backed inflation cycle and fiscal stimulus offset the lower GDP headline, and the eurozone's services sector collapse is not fully discounted.

factors.horizon_split: Both horizons bearish EUR/JPY, reflecting the eurozone's growth collapse outweighing the inflation-driven rate response. Tactical (60) edges strategic (55) because the near-term services PMI shock is visible and impactful.

factors.weight_adjustment_reason: EUR/JPY is a moderate risk-sensitivity cross with +0.68 equity correlation; boost risk factor weight to better reflect pair's sensitivity to risk appetite dynamics

tactical.bilateral_analysis: EUR/JPY is a moderate-risk-sensitivity cross pair with a meaningful equity correlation. The pair has a +0.68 correlation with S&P 500, with equity rallies driving EUR/JPY higher through carry trade acceleration. VIX at 17.4 sits in the goldilocks zone — neither risk-on enough to push EUR/JPY aggressively higher via carry allocation, nor risk-off enough to trigger JPY safe-haven flows. The complication: JPY's safe-haven status is partially impaired during the Iran energy shock (CHF outperformed as the cleaner haven), so the traditional 'VIX spikes → EUR/JPY drops' reflex is dampened. The Nikkei VI at ~29.5 is materially elevated versus VIX, indicating Japan-specific stress. The 30-day SPX-Nikkei correlation at 0.15 is unusually low, confirming Japan-specific dynamics dominate. Net, the risk environment is balanced for EUR/JPY — no clear directional signal.
key_bilateral_metrics: VIX: 17.4 (goldilocks zone)
Nikkei VI: ~29.5 (Japan-specific stress premium)
EUR/JPY-equity correlation: ~+0.68 with S&P 500
SPX-Nikkei correlation: 0.15 (low, Japan-specific dynamics)
CHF outperforming JPY as safe haven in current regime
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price benign risk appetite. The mispricing: if Hormuz re-escalates and VIX spikes above 25, EUR/JPY would initially hold (impaired JPY safe haven) before falling if the energy shock specifically wrecks Japan's terms of trade.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, once the Iran energy shock fully abates and oil normalises, JPY's classical safe-haven correlation should reassert. Japan's +$3.3 trillion NIIP provides the structural repatriation reflex that EUR cannot match. The carry-trade structural weakness (compressed differentials, cleared shorts, elevated JPY vol) means any risk-off episode would trigger EUR/JPY downside more readily than in 2024. If US recession materialises (30-35% consensus probability), EUR/JPY would be among the worst-performing G10 crosses due to the combined risk-off + carry-unwind dynamic. The normalisation of oil prices from elevated levels would re-activate JPY's classical safe-haven properties and trigger carry trade unwinding that disproportionately affects EUR/JPY.
key_bilateral_metrics: Japan NIIP: +$3.3T (repatriation reflex)
Eurozone current account: narrowing toward 1.0% GDP
Carry-trade structural weakness: carry-to-vol at 0.12 (below threshold)
Risk-off scenario: EUR/JPY -5 to -8% in 2024-style unwind
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price benign risk over 6 months. Strategic EUR/JPY bears benefit from any risk-off episode.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical neutral (55) reflects impaired JPY safe-haven status during the energy shock. Strategic bearish (50) anticipates safe-haven correlation re-assertion as energy normalises.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The net positioning for EUR/JPY is mildly bearish: EUR leveraged funds are light long (+11,594, 15th percentile) while JPY leveraged funds are net long (+56,400, 75th percentile). Combining both legs, the implied EUR/JPY speculative positioning is roughly neutral to short — markets are not chasing the long EUR/JPY carry trade, and active specs are actually positioned for yen appreciation. This is a headwind for EUR/JPY upside. The contrarian implication: the EUR/JPY long trade is not crowded, so downside squeeze risk is limited, but the absence of fresh EUR/JPY long building removes a key support mechanism. Japanese institutional flows (GPIF, lifers) are opaque but post-intervention dynamics suggest Japanese institutions are pausing foreign bond purchases at USD/JPY 156 — reducing structural JPY-selling and supporting the yen side of EUR/JPY.
key_bilateral_metrics: EUR leveraged funds: +11,594 (15th percentile — light)
JPY leveraged funds: +56,400 (75th percentile — moderate long)
Combined EUR/JPY speculative positioning: net neutral-to-short
Japanese institutional foreign bond purchases: likely paused at USD/JPY 156
EUR asset managers: +290,898 (structural long, slow-moving)
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price modest yen-bullish positioning. The contrarian risk for EUR/JPY: if both the ECB and BOJ surprise hawkishly at their June meetings, the combined unwind could create volatile two-way price action.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: The strategic flow picture is mixed. EUR asset manager longs (+290,898) provide structural EUR/JPY support, but JPY leveraged fund longs (+56,400) provide the counterweight. Japanese GPIF rebalancing and lifer foreign-bond hedging decisions are the key strategic flow channels — at USD/JPY below 150, lifers accelerate purchases (JPY-selling, EUR/JPY-supportive), while above 155, purchases pause. Real-money structural flows from reserve diversification into EUR (~20% COFER share) are a slow-burn EUR/JPY positive. The EUR/JPY carry trade community has thinned significantly since the August 2024 unwind, with carry-to-vol too low to attract fresh institutional carry capital.
key_bilateral_metrics: EUR asset managers: +290,898 (structural long)
JPY asset managers: -26,550 (structural short)
Lifer activation level: USD/JPY < 150 (not currently active)
Reserve diversification into EUR: gradual (~+0.5pp/year)
Carry trade structural rebuilding: minimal at current carry-to-vol
strategic.what_is_priced: Strategic positioning is balanced with no strong directional signal from flows alone.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical bearish (60) reflects the net-long-JPY speculative tilt. Strategic neutral (50) reflects the offsetting structural flows.

tactical.combined_positioning_signal: Net neutral-to-short EUR/JPY in speculative community — leveraged funds light long EUR (15th percentile) and moderate long JPY (75th percentile). EUR/JPY long trade is not crowded.
tactical.unwind_trigger: Both ECB and BOJ surprise hawkishly at June meetings, triggering volatile two-way price action and potential rapid repositioning
tactical.historical_analogue: August 2024 carry unwind — when JPY leveraged funds unwound short positions rapidly, EUR/JPY dropped sharply before recovering
tactical.contrarian_case: The EUR/JPY long trade is not crowded (EUR leveraged funds at 15th percentile), meaning a sustained upside surprise in the ECB-BOJ differential could attract significant fresh long positioning that drives EUR/JPY sharply higher, particularly if the BOJ delivers a dovish hold while the ECB hikes aggressively. The absence of crowding limits the downside from a positioning unwind — the bearish call from positioning is headwind, not catalyst.

strategic.combined_positioning_signal: EUR asset manager structural long (+290,898) offsets JPY leveraged fund long (+56,400), creating a balanced strategic positioning picture with no dominant directional signal
strategic.unwind_trigger: EUR/JPY breaks below 180 on BOJ double-hike, triggering asset manager EUR unwind and carry exit simultaneously
strategic.historical_analogue: 2023-2024 yen depreciation episode where institutional EUR/JPY longs were slow to unwind until a major central bank catalyst emerged
strategic.contrarian_case: The EUR asset manager structural long (+290,898 contracts) represents a large slow-moving position that provides a durable floor for EUR/JPY — these positions do not unwind rapidly absent a severe growth or financial shock. A strategic bullish EUR/JPY scenario could develop if both the ECB and BOJ disappoint on their respective hike paths, leaving the carry differential intact and attracting new carry-seeking institutional capital into the pair at lower levels.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: Both the eurozone and Japan are net energy importers, creating a partially offsetting dynamic for EUR/JPY when oil prices move. Brent at ~$108/bbl hurts both economies, but Japan is significantly more exposed: 95.9% Middle East crude dependence versus the eurozone's diversified supply. The net commodity exposure for EUR/JPY: oil rising is negative for both EUR and JPY, but more negative for JPY due to extreme ME dependence — mildly EUR/JPY-positive when oil rises. TTF natural gas at €46.93/MWh hurts the eurozone specifically (Japan uses LNG/JKM, a different but correlated benchmark). The eurozone's goods trade balance has contracted (€11.5bn surplus in February vs €23.1bn a year earlier), while Japan's trade balance shows a similar squeeze (¥667bn surplus in March vs expected ¥1,106bn). Japanese investment stock in the EU is very significant (€212.5 billion), mainly in the Netherlands and Germany, while EU investment in Japan amounts to €86.6 billion. This bilateral investment asymmetry means income flows from EU to Japan (dividends, interest on Japanese FDI in Europe) provide structural JPY support that doesn't appear in the trade balance. Japan is the EU's second-biggest trading partner in Asia, with the total EU-Japan trade balance positive at €10.2 billion in 2023, driven by EU services surplus.
key_bilateral_metrics: Brent: $108/bbl — hurts both, JPY more (95.9% ME dependence vs diversified EU)
Net oil impact on EUR/JPY: mildly positive (JPY hurt more)
TTF: €46.93/MWh — hurts eurozone specifically
Japanese FDI in EU: €212.5bn vs EU FDI in Japan: €86.6bn
EU-Japan bilateral trade balance: €10.2bn EU surplus (2023)
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price the energy shock as mildly EUR/JPY-positive via Japan's larger energy vulnerability. The offset: TTF hitting the eurozone specifically creates a balancing headwind.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, Japan's primary income surplus (~¥45-50T annualised from +$3.3T NIIP) structurally supports the yen and has no eurozone equivalent at scale. The eurozone current account at 1.6% GDP is narrowing, while Japan's current account remains robust at ~4% GDP. If Brent normalises to $80-90 on Iran ceasefire, Japan's terms of trade improve proportionally more than the eurozone's (because Japan's import elasticity to oil is higher), creating a yen-positive impulse that would weigh on EUR/JPY. Trump's 25% auto tariff on EU vehicles hits Germany disproportionately without a direct parallel impact on Japan (Japan's auto tariff is a separate and unresolved risk). The eurozone's structural energy-import vulnerability (gas storage at 33.8% vs 55% norm) creates summer refilling pressure that doesn't apply to Japan. Net, the strategic external balance tilts bearish EUR/JPY as Japan's structural income surplus provides a persistent JPY-supportive force that the eurozone cannot match.
key_bilateral_metrics: Japan current account: ~4% GDP vs eurozone: ~1.6% GDP (narrowing)
Japan NIIP: +$3.3T (no eurozone equivalent at this scale)
Oil normalisation scenario (Brent $80): JPY benefits more than EUR
Trump 25% auto tariff: hurts eurozone (Germany) specifically
EU gas storage deficit: 21.2pp below norm (summer refilling pressure)
strategic.what_is_priced: Strategic markets underweight the primary income asymmetry. Japan's structural income surplus is a persistent JPY-supportive force that the eurozone cannot match.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical neutral (55) reflects the partially offsetting energy-importer dynamic. Strategic bearish (60) reflects Japan's dominant primary income surplus and the asymmetric terms-of-trade recovery path.

net_commodity_exposure.base_commodity_profile: EUR — net energy importer (oil and gas); TTF natural gas at €46.93/MWh is a direct cost; diversified oil supply reduces Middle East dependency versus Japan
net_commodity_exposure.quote_commodity_profile: JPY — net energy importer with 95.9% Middle East crude dependence; LNG/JKM benchmark for gas; significantly more exposed to oil price shocks than eurozone
net_commodity_exposure.net_effect_calculation: Step 1: Oil rises ($108/bbl elevated) — both EUR and JPY negatively affected as net importers. Step 2: JPY hurt proportionally more due to 95.9% ME crude dependence versus eurozone's diversified supply. Step 3: Net oil-up effect on EUR/JPY is mildly positive (JPY weakens more than EUR). Step 4: TTF natural gas at €46.93/MWh creates a eurozone-specific cost not matched by Japan's LNG exposure — partially offsets the oil-positive for EUR/JPY. Step 5: Net commodity effect on EUR/JPY at current prices is approximately neutral with a mild positive bias (oil effect > TTF offset). Step 6: If Brent normalises to $80-90, Japan's terms of trade improve more than eurozone's, reversing the current mild EUR/JPY-positive to EUR/JPY-negative.
net_commodity_exposure.scenario_oil_up_10pct: Brent +10% (~$119/bbl): JPY weakens more than EUR due to higher import vulnerability; EUR/JPY mildly positive, estimated +0.5 to +1.0 big figures, partially offset by TTF pressure on eurozone
net_commodity_exposure.scenario_oil_down_10pct: Brent -10% (~$97/bbl): JPY benefits more than EUR from terms-of-trade improvement; EUR/JPY mildly negative, estimated -0.5 to -1.0 big figures, as Japan's disproportionate energy import reduction outweighs eurozone benefit
net_commodity_exposure.dominant_commodity_driver: Brent crude oil — the primary commodity driver for this pair due to Japan's extreme Middle East dependence (95.9%) creating asymmetric sensitivity versus the eurozone's diversified energy supply

tactical.bilateral_analysis: Both central banks have pivoted hawkish, creating an unusual bilateral equilibrium. The ECB's April 30 communication pre-positioned for a June hike with Lagarde acknowledging 'upside risks intensified,' while Kazimir stated a June hike is 'all but inevitable.' The BOJ's April 28 communication was even more hawkish: three dissenters for an immediate hike, Ueda stating 'real interest rates are at significantly low levels,' and the Outlook Report revising FY2026 core CPI to 2.8%. The credibility differential: the ECB's hawkish communication is market-validated (OIS pricing aligns with guidance), while the BOJ's hawkishness is partially discounted given decades of communication opacity. The market trusts the ECB to follow through on a June hike more than it trusts the BOJ to surprise with a June hike — this trust asymmetry provides mild EUR/JPY support tactically. The 125-basis-point yield advantage favours the Euro, and the outlook remains cautiously bullish, supported by a dominant daily uptrend and a favourable interest rate differential.
key_bilateral_metrics: ECB June hike probability: ~81% (market-validated)
BOJ June hike probability: ~35-40% (partially discounted)
ECB hawks vocal: Kazimir, Nagel, Holzmann, Müller
BOJ hawks: Nakagawa, Takata, Tamura (3 of 9 dissenting)
Credibility trust asymmetry: ECB > BOJ (historical opacity discount)
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price the ECB delivering with higher probability than the BOJ — this is mildly EUR/JPY-supportive. The mispricing: the BOJ's June hike probability may be too low given three dissenters.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, the BOJ's credibility is strengthening while the ECB's faces a growth-constraint test. The BOJ has three votes for 1.00%, the Outlook Report has locked in 2.8% FY2026 CPI, and the Shunto wage cycle provides structural cover. The ECB's hike path faces a credibility test: can Lagarde deliver multiple hikes into a services PMI recession? The 2003-style 'hike into weakness' precedent exists (Trichet in 2008), but the political cost was severe. If the ECB delivers one hike but pauses, while the BOJ delivers two hikes, the credibility-implied EUR/JPY direction is lower. The BOJ is transitioning from opacity to transparency, which reduces the discount applied to its guidance over the strategic horizon, making its hawkish signals more market-credible.
key_bilateral_metrics: BOJ FY2026 CPI: 2.8% (highest projection revision ever)
ECB staff GDP 2026: 0.9% (services PMI at odds)
BOJ normalisation velocity: accelerating
ECB normalisation sustainability: questionable at services PMI < 47
Communication regime: BOJ becoming more transparent; ECB maintaining data-dependence
strategic.what_is_priced: Strategic markets price ECB credibility as superior to BOJ. We see this gap narrowing, which is bearish EUR/JPY.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical neutral (60) reflects balanced dual-hawkishness. Strategic bearish (60) reflects the BOJ's accelerating credibility versus the ECB's growth-constrained hike path.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The near-term event calendar is densely populated with EUR/JPY-specific catalysts. The ECB meeting (4-5 June) and BOJ meeting (16-17 June) fall within 12 days of each other, creating a binary sequence where the ECB sets the tone and the BOJ provides the follow-through. Trump's 25% auto tariff on EU vehicles (effective ~8 May) specifically hurts Germany without a parallel Japan impact (yet), creating a EUR-negative asymmetry. The Iran ceasefire trajectory affects both currencies but JPY more acutely — a ceasefire failure (Brent $130) would be EUR/JPY-positive via Japanese terms-of-trade damage. MoF intervention risk at USD/JPY 160 caps EUR/JPY upside through the USD/JPY channel. French political risk (OAT-Bund at 64bp) is a EUR-specific negative with no JPY parallel. Net tactical event risk tilts mildly bearish EUR/JPY because the EU-specific events (auto tariff, French risk) are incremental negatives while Japan-specific events (BOJ, intervention) are more balanced.
key_bilateral_metrics: ECB meeting: 4-5 June (binary; 81% hike probability)
BOJ meeting: 16-17 June (binary; ~35-40% hike probability)
Trump auto tariff: 25% on EU (effective ~8 May); hurts EUR specifically
MoF intervention ceiling: USD/JPY 160 (caps EUR/JPY upside)
Iran ceasefire: fragile — failure more EUR/JPY-positive, success more neutral
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price moderate event risk. The mispricing: the cumulative probability of EU-specific negative events (auto tariff + French political) is underweighted.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, event risks are bilateral: Iran ceasefire trajectory, Trump trade policy (could target both EU and Japan on autos), and central bank delivery sequences. The net geopolitical impact on EUR/JPY is balanced over 6 months — EU-specific risks (French fiscal, trade war) offset Japan-specific risks (energy dependence, potential auto tariffs). The combined bilateral event risk creates roughly symmetric tail outcomes over the 6-month horizon, making geopolitics a neutral rather than directional factor at the strategic level.
key_bilateral_metrics: Iran ceasefire 6M holding probability: ~55%
Trump Japan auto tariff probability: ~18%
French political accident probability: ~30%
Combined bilateral event risk: balanced
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price contained geopolitical risk for both currencies. Strategic event risk is approximately balanced for EUR/JPY.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical bearish (55) reflects the near-term EU-specific event concentration. Strategic neutral (50) reflects the balanced bilateral distribution over 6 months.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: Japan's 250% gross debt/GDP versus the eurozone's heterogeneous fiscal picture (Germany 63.5%, France 115.6%, Italy 137.1%) creates a complex bilateral comparison. Japan's fiscal risk is contained by BOJ holdings (~50% of JGBs) and domestic ownership (~90%), while the eurozone faces fragmentation risk (BTP-Bund at 78bp, OAT-Bund at 64bp). Neither fiscal regime is currently material to EUR/JPY at the tactical horizon. Takaichi's fiscal stimulus is mildly JPY-negative (supply pressure on JGBs without adequate demand) but the rate-normalisation offset (higher JGB yields attract domestic capital) is roughly neutralising. The TPI backstop exists for eurozone fragmentation but activation would itself be a EUR-negative signal.
key_bilateral_metrics: Japan gross debt/GDP: ~250% vs eurozone weighted average: ~90%
Japan domestic JGB ownership: ~90% vs eurozone fragmentation risk
BTP-Bund: 78bp; OAT-Bund: 64bp (contained but elevated)
Japan fiscal stimulus: ¥21.3T supplementary
Germany fiscal expansion: €500bn infrastructure + €83bn defence
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price both fiscal regimes as non-material to the pair. Appropriate at tactical horizon.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, France's fiscal trajectory is the dominant EUR-specific risk, while Japan's fiscal-monetary tension (BOJ normalisation raising debt-servicing costs) is the JPY-specific risk. These partially offset. A French ratings downgrade (40% probability by year-end) would widen OAT-Bund and weigh on EUR. A JGB 10Y break above 3% would raise fiscal sustainability concerns and potentially slow BOJ normalisation — but this is also JPY-bearish via the fiscal channel. Net: balanced strategic fiscal risk for EUR/JPY. Each 100bp JGB yield rise equates to approximately 2% GDP in additional debt-servicing costs, creating a structural tension in the BOJ normalisation path that limits the pace of yen appreciation via the rate channel.
key_bilateral_metrics: France downgrade probability: ~40% (EUR-negative)
JGB 10Y 3% break probability: ~30% (JPY-mixed)
TPI backstop: available but activation EUR-negative signal
Japan fiscal-monetary tension: each 100bp JGB yield rise = ~2% GDP debt-servicing cost
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price contained fiscal risk for both currencies. This is broadly appropriate but underweights the French tail.

factors.horizon_split: Both horizons neutral, reflecting the genuinely balanced bilateral fiscal dynamics.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: EUR/JPY's technical structure from price data: spot at 184.65 sits just above the 100-day SMA (184.32) but below the 50-day SMA (185.32), with the 200-day SMA at 182.66. The MA configuration is moderately bullish (50d > 100d > 200d) but spot's slip below the 50d is a near-term warning. The pair rallied from 155.59 to 187.96 over 13 months — a massive trending move — and the recent pullback to 182.38 tested the 100d before bouncing. In the bigger picture, the pullback from 187.93 is steep with no sign of reversal yet; the uptrend from 114.42 is still expected to resume targeting 194.88, but a sustained break of the 55-week EMA at 177.76 would argue for a medium-term downtrend toward 175.41. The pair's realised volatility (elevated at ~11-13% post-intervention) exceeds implied (~10-11%), favouring spot expression over options. The vol term structure is in mild backwardation through the June ECB-BOJ event cluster. Key pair-specific technicals: support at 183.34, 182.01, 180.78; resistance at 185.82, 187.96, 190.00 (round number). The 185 area is now the key tactical battle zone — a close above 185.82 (61.8% Fibonacci) would confirm the uptrend's resumption.
key_bilateral_metrics: 52-week range: 155.59-187.96; percentile: 90th
tactical.what_is_priced: The daily technical signal for EUR/JPY across multiple moving average and indicator timeframes shows 'Strong Buy' on several timeframes, with 12 Buy signals and 0 Sell signals from moving averages. This is consistent with the long-term trend but may be overstating near-term bullishness given the 50d breach.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: The strategic structure spans a wide range (177-188) with the 200d SMA at 182.66 providing the structural floor. The 13-month uptrend from 155.59 is intact as long as 180.78 holds. The strategic implied vol range (~10-12%) suggests a 6M outcome distribution of approximately 170-198, wide enough to encompass both the bullish (carry + ECB hike) and bearish (BOJ normalisation + risk-off) scenarios. The factor weight (0.03) limits synthesis impact. The medium-term technical picture remains constructive on the long-term trend but the near-term breach of the 50d SMA introduces uncertainty about the pace of any resumption.
key_bilateral_metrics: Strategic range: 177-188
200d SMA trajectory: rising (~182.66)
Medium-term uptrend support: 180.78
6M implied range: ~170-198
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price a mild downward drift from current levels. This is broadly consistent with the structural technical picture.

factors.horizon_split: Both horizons neutral, reflecting the competing long-term uptrend versus near-term 50d breach. Tactical (55) edges strategic (50) due to better-defined near-term levels.

expression_assessment.vol_landscape_summary: EUR/JPY IV estimated at 10-11% for 1M ATM, mid-range at the 55-65th percentile of its one-year distribution. Realised volatility (~11-13%) slightly exceeds implied, creating a negative variance risk premium. Vol term structure is in mild backwardation through the June ECB-BOJ event cluster.
expression_assessment.directional_expression: Spot expression is preferred given RV > IV. For EUR/JPY-bullish views: long spot at 183.50-184.00 with stop at 182.00 and target at 187-188. For EUR/JPY-bearish views: short spot at 186-187 with stop at 188.50 and target at 182-183. Options: 1M EUR/JPY puts struck at 182 offer asymmetric payoff into the BOJ June meeting; selling 188 calls against intervention ceiling generates income.
expression_assessment.event_timing_consideration: ECB 4-5 June and BOJ 16-17 June fall within 12 days of each other, creating a binary sequential event structure. Vol backwardation through June argues against naked vol-selling strategies. Event straddles or ratio spreads may be appropriate for the June window.
expression_assessment.liquidity_note: EUR/JPY is a liquid major cross pair with typical spreads of 0.5-1.5 pips in normal conditions. Liquidity may widen around the June ECB-BOJ event cluster and around any MoF intervention episodes in the USD/JPY market that transmit to EUR/JPY.

factors.weight_adjustment_reason: Non-USD cross — USD funding is a secondary vehicle channel for EUR/JPY; reduce weight by 0.03 at both horizons

tactical.bilateral_analysis: For EUR/JPY as a cross pair, USD liquidity operates through the vehicle currency channel — most EUR/JPY transactions settle through USD, making USD funding conditions relevant. The Fed at 3.50-3.75% with dovish bias affects both currencies similarly: a Fed cut would weaken USD, benefiting both EUR and JPY, with the net EUR/JPY impact depending on which currency's USD correlation is stronger. Historically, EUR/USD has a higher beta to Fed moves than USD/JPY, which would be mildly EUR/JPY-positive on a Fed cut. The EUR/USD cross-currency basis (not found specifically) and JPY/USD basis (structurally negative at ~-30 to -50bp historically) both affect the all-in cost of funding EUR/JPY positions. The dollar smile framework places USD in the 'stagflation middle' — neither aggressively strong nor weak — creating a muted vehicle-currency impact on the cross. For EUR/JPY specifically, USD funding conditions are a secondary factor.
key_bilateral_metrics: Fed funds: 3.50-3.75% (dovish bias)
EUR/USD beta to Fed moves: moderate-to-high
USD/JPY beta to Fed moves: moderate
JPY/USD cross-currency basis: ~-30 to -50bp (structurally negative)
Dollar smile position: stagflation middle (muted cross impact)
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price benign USD funding. For EUR/JPY, the USD funding channel is secondary to the direct ECB-BOJ differential.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, Fed cuts compress USD funding costs, which reduces the cost of intermediating EUR/JPY through the USD channel. This is marginally supportive of EUR/JPY liquidity but does not create directional bias. The dominant strategic USD-related factor for EUR/JPY is reserve diversification: EUR's rising share (~20%) versus JPY's declining share (~5-6%) creates a mild structural EUR/JPY bid from reserve manager rebalancing, but the pace is glacial. Net, USD liquidity is a neutral factor for EUR/JPY at both horizons, and the weight has been reduced from default for this non-USD cross.
key_bilateral_metrics: Fed cut probability H2 2026: ~50-75bp
EUR reserve share: ~20% (rising)
JPY reserve share: ~5-6% (stable-to-declining)
Reserve rebalancing EUR/JPY impact: very marginal
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price moderate Fed easing. The EUR/JPY impact is secondary.

factors.horizon_split: Both horizons neutral at low confidence (55/50), reflecting the muted vehicle-currency channel for this cross pair.

adjustments.reason: EUR/JPY is a moderate risk-sensitivity cross with +0.68 equity correlation; boost risk factor weight to better reflect pair's sensitivity to risk appetite dynamics. Strategic weight boosted from 0.05 to 0.08.
Non-USD cross — USD funding is a secondary vehicle channel for EUR/JPY; reduce tactical weight from 0.11 to 0.08. Strategic weight reduced from 0.12 to 0.09. Net weight sum preserved at 1.00.

tactical.score_interpretation: Net score of -0.196 maps to the Neutral-to-mildly-bearish zone. With six factors neutral and three bearish (Inflation/Growth, Flows, Event Risk) and none bullish, the distribution tilts slightly bearish but not with sufficient force to assign a confident Bearish stance. Conviction is 2 (low) because the bearish factors carry moderate confidence and the dominant Policy & Rates factor is explicitly neutral.
tactical.rationale: The tactical EUR/JPY setup is genuinely conflicted — a rare configuration where both dossiers point bullish on their respective currencies, creating an offsetting pair signal. The ECB's hawkish pivot (81% June hike probability) is normally EUR/JPY-positive, but the BOJ's unprecedented 6-3 hawkish vote with three dissenters at 1.00% creates a competing JPY-positive force from the same factor. The weighted scoring resolves this tension as neutral on Policy & Rates (+0.000) — the dominant factor. The bearish tilt comes from Inflation & Growth (-0.108), where the eurozone's services PMI collapse (47.4) and IFO at COVID lows represent a more acute growth shock than Japan's comparatively resilient data (composite PMI 52.4, Tankan solid). Flows & Positioning contributes -0.060 via the net-long-JPY speculative configuration. The pair is trading at the 90th percentile of its 52-week range, which creates natural mean-reversion pressure. The path of least resistance is sideways-to-lower: EUR/JPY likely consolidates in the 182-186 range with a mild downward bias as the June ECB-BOJ meeting sequence approaches. The pair lacks a unidirectional catalyst — the ECB meeting (4-5 June) could rally EUR/JPY to 186-187 on a hawkish hike, only for the BOJ meeting (16-17 June) to reverse it if a surprise hike materialises. Conviction is low (2) because the bilateral dynamics are genuinely balanced and the binary event outcomes are symmetric. The difference from standalone dossier views: combining a 'Bullish EUR' and 'Bullish JPY' dossier produces a near-neutral pair signal with mild bearish tilt — the pair analysis adds value by revealing the offset that isn't visible in either standalone view. Risk appetite (Factor 3) is tactically neutral, consistent with VIX at 17.4 and the partially impaired JPY safe-haven status during the Iran energy shock. Guidance & Credibility (Factor 6) is also neutral, as both central banks have pivoted hawkish with the ECB more trusted to deliver. The pair's PPP overvaluation of ~33% above fair value provides structural gravity that increases the asymmetry of downside scenarios relative to upside.
tactical.g10_relative_ranking: EUR/JPY ranks in the middle of the JPY-cross opportunity set — behind short AUD/JPY and short NZD/JPY (where the rate-convergence + risk-off dynamic is cleaner), roughly equal to short GBP/JPY, and ahead of short USD/JPY (where the carry cost is higher). Among EUR crosses, EUR/JPY is less attractive than long EUR/USD (where the rate-convergence channel is one-directional) or short EUR/CHF (where the safe-haven dynamic is cleaner). The pair's bilateral neutrality makes it a lower-conviction expression of either standalone currency view.

conditions: ECB hike is confirmed but with cautious framing on growth
BOJ stays in hawkish hold posture with no dissent escalation
Risk appetite remains benign (VIX stays below 22)
base_case.path: EUR/JPY consolidates in 182-186 range over 2-4 weeks, ending around 183-184. Net result: modest EUR/JPY downside of 50-100 pips from current spot of 184.65.

conditions: ECB delivers a hawkish hike with explicit guidance for more tightening
BOJ June holds with no dissent escalation beyond current three dissenters
BOJ delivers June surprise hike to 1.00% with 5-4 vote
ECB delivers cautious hike with dovish framing on growth outlook

tactical.path_expectation: The 2-4 week path is likely a choppy consolidation within the 182-186 range, with a mild downward drift. Phase 1 (now to late May): EUR/JPY holds 183-185 as markets digest the post-intervention recovery and await the June meeting sequence. The pair will face resistance at 185.32 (50d SMA) and 185.82 (61.8% Fibonacci) on any bounces. Phase 2 (late May to 5 June): pre-ECB positioning lifts EUR/JPY to test 185-186 as the high-probability ECB hike gets priced with increasing precision. Phase 3 (5-17 June): ECB outcome drives initial reaction (+/-100-150 pips), then BOJ meeting provides the second binary — if the BOJ surprises with a hike, the ECB-driven gains are likely reversed and then some. Expect daily ranges of 80-120 pips with 2-3 event-driven spikes. The pair will not resolve directionally until both June meetings have passed and the combined policy-delivery picture is clear.

increase_conditions: Increase to Bearish conviction 3 if BOJ Summary of Opinions shows 4+ members supporting imminent hike AND Tokyo CPI (23 May) prints above 2.0%
Increase to Bearish conviction 4 if BOJ delivers June hike AND ECB hike is cautious/dovish — differential compression confirmed
decrease_conditions: Decrease to conviction 1 (Neutral) if BOJ June hold with no dissent escalation AND ECB hawkish hike with guidance for further tightening
Shift to Bullish conviction 3 if ECB delivers hawkish hike AND BOJ holds dovishly AND Brent rises above $115 (JPY hurt disproportionately)

risk_reward.risk_reward_ratio: 1.0:1 base vs upside; 1.5:1 downside vs upside
risk_reward.expected_value: Probability-weighted EV approximately -20 pips (~-0.1%), reflecting roughly symmetric distribution with mild bearish tilt
risk_reward.asymmetry_assessment: Roughly symmetric distribution at the tactical horizon; no strong edge from the near-term setup. The binary June event sequence creates fat tails in both directions that approximately cancel at current implied probabilities.

strategic.score_interpretation: Net score of -0.407 maps to a moderately Bearish EUR/JPY stance over the strategic horizon. Five factors bearish (1, 2, 3, 5, 6) and five neutral — no factors bullish. Conviction remains at 2 (low) because the confidence levels across factors are generally moderate (50-60) and the pair's extreme PPP overvaluation creates strong mean-reversion tailwinds that the strategic horizon begins to capture.
strategic.rationale: The strategic EUR/JPY outlook tilts bearish as the bilateral rate-convergence trajectory shifts to favour JPY. Over 6 months, the BOJ's normalisation path is more durable and less constrained than the ECB's: Japan's wage-price cycle (Shunto 5.09%) provides structural inflation persistence that enables sustained hiking, while the ECB faces a services recession that may limit cumulative hikes. The projected end-2026 differential of +100-125bp (potentially narrower than today's +125bp) is consistent with EUR/JPY drifting toward 178-182. External Balance contributes the largest single bearish factor at the strategic horizon (-0.096): Japan's +$3.3T NIIP and ~4% GDP current account surplus structurally support JPY, while the eurozone's narrowing current account and energy vulnerability weigh on EUR. Risk Appetite shifts bearish as oil normalisation re-activates JPY's safe-haven correlation. The ~33% PPP overvaluation provides the structural gravity — at these extreme deviations, any catalyst that triggers mean-reversion (BOJ hike, risk-off, oil normalisation) has outsized directional impact. Guidance & Credibility (-0.072) reflects the BOJ's accelerating credibility relative to the ECB's growth-constrained hike path: the BOJ has the inflation and wage data to support further normalisation, while the ECB is hiking into a services PMI recession with credibility risk on the upside. Conviction is limited to 2 because (a) both central banks are tightening, creating genuine bilateral uncertainty, (b) the carry dynamic still mildly supports EUR/JPY longs (positive carry for the foreseeable horizon), and (c) the pair has defied PPP gravity for years and could continue to do so if risk appetite stays robust. The path is a gradual stair-step lower rather than a sharp collapse, with each BOJ hike delivery providing the sequential catalysts for the strategic decline. Flows & Positioning is neutral strategically because the offsetting structural flows (EUR asset manager longs vs JPY leveraged fund longs) roughly cancel, leaving no dominant directional flow signal.
strategic.g10_relative_ranking: EUR/JPY ranks 5th most attractive short among JPY crosses (behind short AUD/JPY, short NZD/JPY, short GBP/JPY, and roughly equal to short CAD/JPY). The pair is less clean as a JPY-long expression than short USD/JPY because the EUR side has its own hawkish momentum. Among EUR shorts, it is more attractive than short EUR/USD (where the EUR-bullish thesis is stronger) but less attractive than short EUR/CHF (where the safe-haven dynamic is one-directional). Strategic EUR/JPY shorts should be sized smaller than a pure JPY-long expression given the offsetting EUR-bullish dynamics.

conditions: ECB delivers 50bp of hikes (June + one more), BOJ delivers 50bp (June or July + one more), differential stable at +100-125bp
10Y Bund-JGB spread compresses from 56bp toward 40bp as JGB yields continue rising
Risk appetite remains broadly neutral — no severe risk-off but no strong risk-on either
base_case.path: EUR/JPY drifts to 180-182 by Q4 2026 through a phased decline: consolidation in 182-186 around June meetings, directional drift lower to 180-182 by September as BOJ normalisation path firms, potential acceleration through 180 if BOJ delivers second hike or risk-off materialises. The decline is a stair-step grind rather than a sharp unwind because the carry dynamic provides a floor.

conditions: ECB delivers 75bp+ of hikes while BOJ delivers only 25bp, widening the differential toward +150bp
Iran ceasefire fails pushing oil to $120+, hurting JPY terms of trade disproportionately and sustaining carry demand
BOJ delivers 50bp of hikes by September (June or July + September), compressing the differential to +75-100bp
ECB capitulates after one hike on growth concerns while a risk-off episode triggers carry unwind and oil normalises to $80

strategic.path_expectation: The strategic path is a phased decline over 1-6 months. Phase 1 (May-June): consolidation in 182-186 around the dual June meetings, ending with a net modest decline if both central banks deliver as expected. The ECB hike (4-5 June) provides a temporary rally to 185-187 before the BOJ meeting (16-17 June) either validates the bearish thesis (surprise hike) or delays it (hold). Phase 2 (July-September): directional drift lower as the BOJ normalisation path firms and the carry-to-vol ratio continues to decline, targeting the 180-182 area. Each BOJ meeting without a hike becomes a de facto delay rather than a cancellation, as the three-dissenter configuration creates constant pressure. Phase 3 (October-November): potential acceleration through 180 if the BOJ delivers a second hike or a risk-off episode materialises, with the 178 area providing medium-term structural support. Pullback levels for re-entry into strategic shorts: 186-187. The decline is more likely to be a stair-step grind than a sharp unwind because the carry dynamic provides a floor that prevents outright collapse absent a severe risk-off event.

increase_conditions: Increase to Bearish conviction 3 if BOJ delivers June or July hike with 5-4 or tighter vote AND eurozone services PMI stays below 48 for two consecutive months
Increase to Bearish conviction 4 if BOJ delivers two hikes by Q3 AND ECB pauses after one hike AND Brent sustained below $90
decrease_conditions: Decrease to Neutral conviction 1 if ECB delivers 75bp+ of hikes AND BOJ delays to 2027 AND risk-on regime sustains above 50 on services PMI
Shift to Bullish if ECB-BOJ differential widens past +175bp (requires ECB hiking aggressively without BOJ follow-through over multiple meetings)

risk_reward.risk_reward_ratio: 0.7:1 base vs upside; 1.5:1 downside vs upside
risk_reward.expected_value: (50% × 350 pips) + (25% × 800 pips) + (25% × -450 pips) = 175 + 200 - 112.5 = +262.5 pips (~1.4%) probability-weighted expected value
risk_reward.asymmetry_assessment: Moderately favourable asymmetry at the strategic horizon — the downside tail (carry unwind + BOJ double-hike scenario) at 175-178 is larger in magnitude than the upside tail (188-190), creating positive expected value despite the balanced scenario probabilities. The fat left tail from PPP mean-reversion and BOJ acceleration is the key asymmetric driver.

consensus_divergence.bank_consensus_forecast: ExchangeRates.org.uk aggregated bank forecasts: EUR/JPY 184.25 by June 2026, 182.27 by September 2026, 180.48 by December 2026. LongForecast algorithmic projections: 185 end-May, 186 end-June, 191 year-end. PandaForecast December target ~176.
consensus_divergence.our_tactical_view: Neutral, targeting 183-184 over 2-4 weeks with mild bearish tilt; consolidation in 182-186 range
consensus_divergence.our_strategic_view: Bearish, targeting 180-182 by Q4 2026 with downside case of 175-178
consensus_divergence.divergence_direction: Below consensus for LongForecast and algorithmic projections; broadly in-line with bank consensus (ExchangeRates.org.uk)
consensus_divergence.divergence_magnitude: ~500-700 pips below LongForecast year-end target of 191; broadly aligned with ExchangeRates.org.uk bank consensus at ~180.48 December target
consensus_divergence.why_we_differ: We diverge from the bullish algorithmic consensus (LongForecast, CoinCodex) because we weight BOJ normalisation velocity and eurozone growth deterioration more heavily. The algorithmic models appear to weight the carry differential and historical trend momentum heavily, which favours EUR/JPY upside given the +125bp carry and 13-month uptrend. We instead weight the trajectory of the differential (decelerating ECB, accelerating BOJ), the PPP gravity of 33% overvaluation, and the services PMI recession signal in the eurozone as more fundamentally important at the strategic horizon than momentum factors.
consensus_divergence.payoff_if_right: Capture 250-450 pips of strategic downside versus LongForecast consensus; approximately aligned with bank consensus
consensus_divergence.risk_if_wrong: Miss 300-500 pips of upside if the carry-supported uptrend resumes and both central banks deliver fewer hikes than expected

cross_horizon_consistency.explanation: Tactical and Strategic horizons diverge mildly — the tactical horizon lacks a clear catalyst until the June meeting sequence, while the strategic horizon allows the BOJ normalisation path to play out over multiple meetings. Both horizons carry conviction 2, but the tactical setup is genuinely balanced (net score -0.196) while the strategic setup shows clearer directional bias (net score -0.407). The June ECB-BOJ meeting sequence is the inflection point that could align both horizons.
cross_horizon_consistency.inflection_point: June 4-17, 2026: ECB meeting (4-5 June) + BOJ meeting (16-17 June) — if the BOJ surprises hawkish, both horizons align bearish EUR/JPY; if the ECB dominates, the tactical view could flip to mildly bullish while the strategic view moderates
cross_horizon_consistency.trading_implication: Tactical positioning should be light and event-sensitive with tight stops (182.00 stop for short positions, 188.50 stop for long positions). Strategic positioning can be built gradually on EUR/JPY rallies toward 186-187 with wider stops at 189, targeting the 180-182 zone by Q4 2026.

forward_vs_spot.forward_1m: ~184.55 (points approximately -10 pips, reflecting +125bp carry advantage to EUR)
forward_vs_spot.edge_3m: Approximately -35 to -135 pips below forward-implied rate — modest edge
forward_vs_spot.edge_6m: Approximately 195-395 pips below forward-implied rate of ~183.95 — meaningful capturable edge via short EUR/JPY spot or EUR/JPY put options

catalysts.rationale: The 25% tariff on EU autos hurts Germany specifically without a direct parallel Japan impact, widening the growth divergence that is EUR/JPY-bearish. The auto sector is ~10% of EU goods exports, making this a meaningful EUR-negative shock with no offsetting JPY impact. Germany's outsized exposure within the eurozone creates a fragmentation dimension — ECB policy cannot address asymmetric tariff shocks efficiently.

Strong GDP supports the BOJ hike thesis and is EUR/JPY-bearish via the JPY appreciation channel; weak GDP could delay BOJ normalisation and is EUR/JPY-bullish. The Polymarket-implied 38% probability of 0.9-1.1% YoY growth suggests meaningful uncertainty. A strong print would reinforce the bilateral growth differential already favouring Japan versus the eurozone's contraction-level services PMI, amplifying the bearish EUR/JPY case.

Tokyo CPI is the BOJ's leading indicator for national CPI trends. A print above 2.0% would sharply increase June hike probability, weighing on EUR/JPY through the rate-convergence channel. Consensus expects ~1.5-1.7%; an upside surprise to 2.0%+ would materially shift market pricing for the BOJ June meeting, which is currently at ~35-40% hike probability. Given the BOJ's three-dissenter configuration, a hawkish CPI print could quickly push June hike probability to 50%+.

Higher HICP supports the ECB hike case and is EUR/JPY-bullish via the rate channel, but deepens stagflation concerns by worsening the growth-inflation tradeoff. Lower HICP moderates hike expectations and is EUR/JPY-bearish. The key interaction: if HICP falls while services PMI also falls, the ECB faces a stagflation-lite scenario where hiking becomes harder to justify, removing the primary EUR/JPY support pillar at the tactical horizon.

With 81% probability of +25bp hike priced, a hawkish hike would rally EUR/JPY 100-150 pips on confirmation; a dovish hold would drop EUR/JPY 200-300 pips on pricing collapse. The framing is the critical binary: a hike with guidance for more is EUR/JPY-bullish; a hike described as a pause would be EUR/JPY-neutral-to-bearish. The ECB meeting sets the EUR leg's direction before the BOJ meeting 12 days later, making it the first half of the decisive bilateral event sequence.

Three dissenters at the April meeting create ~40-50% hike probability for June. A hike with a 5-4 vote would drop EUR/JPY 200-400 pips as the rate-convergence thesis accelerates; a hold maintains the current consolidation trajectory but with continued hawkish pressure. This is the most important single bilateral catalyst for EUR/JPY in the 2-4 week window, as it determines whether the BOJ normalisation path is accelerating or stable. A June hike would compress the ECB-BOJ differential trajectory for the first time and trigger a reassessment of the strategic EUR/JPY bearish case's timing.

The Fed decision affects both currencies through the USD vehicle channel; a dovish outcome mildly benefits both EUR and JPY, with the net EUR/JPY impact depending on relative betas. Historically, EUR has a higher beta to Fed moves than JPY, suggesting a dovish Fed is mildly EUR/JPY-positive. However, the direct EUR/JPY impact from a Fed hold or cut is secondary to the ECB-BOJ meeting outcomes occurring in close proximity. The FOMC meeting is overshadowed by the bilateral central bank catalysts in this particular 2-week window.

If June was a hold, July becomes the dominant hike candidate with the quarterly Outlook Report update providing the vehicle for revised guidance. Combined June+July hike probability is ~70%, meaning if June is skipped, July's probability rises substantially. A July hike with updated FY2026 CPI projections (potentially revised above 2.8%) would confirm the BOJ normalisation acceleration and trigger EUR/JPY selling through both the rate-convergence and carry-to-vol channels. The Outlook Report would also provide the forward guidance that shapes the strategic interest rate path for H2 2026.

The first post-summer ECB meeting with new staff projections validates or contradicts the hike path established in June. If growth deterioration forces a pause, this is EUR/JPY-bearish by removing the ECB-bullish support and widening the credibility gap with the BOJ. If the ECB delivers a second hike, the differential widens and EUR/JPY receives support. The outcome depends critically on summer eurozone PMI data and the evolution of the services sector recession dynamic through July-August.

Candidate for the second BOJ hike if June or July delivered the first. A September hike would push the BOJ rate to 1.25% and further compress the ECB-BOJ differential below +100bp if the ECB has not delivered a second hike. This would represent a meaningful acceleration of the rate-convergence thesis and trigger EUR/JPY downside through both the carry-compression and strategic-repositioning channels. Not substantially priced in current forwards.

invalidators.condition: EUR/JPY breaks above 188.50 and sustains for 3+ consecutive trading days on ECB hawkish surprise without BOJ follow-through
invalidators.mechanism: A sustained break of 188.50 would confirm the rate-differential widening channel dominates the pair; ECB delivering more than priced while BOJ holds would widen the differential toward +150bp, invalidating the rate-convergence bearish thesis and signalling the pair is extending its structural carry-driven uptrend

invalidators.condition: BOJ June 17 meeting delivers a dovish hold (8-1 vote, no dissent escalation) AND ECB delivers a hawkish hike with explicit guidance for more tightening
invalidators.mechanism: If the BOJ dissenter count contracts and the ECB guides for additional hikes, the differential would widen toward +150bp — the exact opposite of the strategic bearish thesis. The credibility asymmetry would reassert in EUR's favour, and carry-trade capital would flow back into EUR/JPY longs
invalidators.monitor_indicator: BOJ meeting statement and vote count; ECB press conference forward guidance

invalidators.condition: Iran ceasefire fails and Brent crude sustains above $130 for 5+ consecutive trading days
invalidators.mechanism: Japan's 95.9% Middle East crude dependence means a Brent spike to $130+ would damage Japan's terms of trade disproportionately relative to the eurozone's diversified supply, triggering yen weakness and EUR/JPY appreciation. At $130, the JPY terms-of-trade damage would overwhelm the BOJ hawkish catalyst

invalidators.condition: Eurozone services PMI recovers above 50.0 for two consecutive monthly prints
invalidators.mechanism: Recovery in services PMI above the 50 expansion/contraction threshold would eliminate the key constraint on ECB hawkishness, allowing the ECB to deliver multiple hikes sustainably. This removes the primary argument for ECB deceleration relative to BOJ normalisation and restores confidence in the eurozone growth-inflation balance that supports EUR
invalidators.monitor_indicator: S&P Global eurozone services PMI (monthly flash and final)

invalidators.condition: CFTC leveraged fund JPY net long position surges above +100,000 contracts AND EUR/JPY fails to break below 182.00
invalidators.mechanism: If JPY speculative longs become overcrowded (above +100k) and EUR/JPY refuses to break lower through 182, the positioning becomes contra-indicative — an overcrowded JPY long with insufficient follow-through creates significant squeeze risk that would lift EUR/JPY and invalidate the positioning bearish thesis
invalidators.monitor_indicator: CFTC weekly Traders in Financial Futures (TFF) report — JPY leveraged funds net

invalidators.condition: 10Y Bund-JGB spread widens back above 80bp on either JGB yield retreat below 2.30% or Bund yield surge above 3.30%
invalidators.mechanism: Long-end spread widening back above 80bp would re-establish the structural carry support that underpinned EUR/JPY through 2024-25. Institutional flows driven by the long-end differential would resume EUR/JPY buying, and the carry-to-vol ratio would improve as the yield gap widens, attracting fresh carry capital back into the pair

signposts.confirms_if: Narrows below +100bp (BOJ hikes faster than ECB)
signposts.invalidates_if: Widens above +150bp (ECB hikes more aggressively than BOJ)
signposts.bilateral_importance: The dominant single driver of EUR/JPY spot dynamics; narrowing confirms the yen-appreciation thesis and validates the strategic bearish view. This indicator directly captures the rate-convergence dynamic that is the primary bearish EUR/JPY thesis over the 6-month horizon.
signposts.source: ECB and BOJ rate decisions at respective policy meetings

signposts.bilateral_importance: Long-end spread drives institutional flow dynamics for EUR/JPY through the carry channel; compression removes a key EUR/JPY support pillar that underpinned the pair through 2024-25. Pension funds and insurance companies calibrate EUR/JPY hedging decisions partly based on this spread.
signposts.source: Trading Economics (Bund and JGB 10Y daily prices)

signposts.confirms_if: Falls below $90/bbl sustained (JPY terms of trade improve, safe-haven reasserts)
signposts.invalidates_if: Rises above $130/bbl sustained (JPY hurt disproportionately, EUR/JPY-bullish)
signposts.bilateral_importance: Both currencies are energy importers but JPY is far more exposed (95.9% ME crude dependence vs eurozone's diversified supply); oil direction creates asymmetric EUR/JPY impact, making oil the key commodity signpost for this cross pair. At $130+, the thesis flips from bearish to bullish EUR/JPY.

signposts.confirms_if: Stays below 48.0 for consecutive months (ECB hike path constrained by growth recession)
signposts.invalidates_if: Recovers above 50.0 for two consecutive months (ECB hike path sustainable, bearish EUR/JPY thesis weakened)
signposts.bilateral_importance: The key constraint on ECB hawkishness; directly limits the EUR-positive rate channel by creating a ceiling on cumulative ECB tightening. A sustained sub-48 reading reinforces the view that the ECB cannot match the BOJ's normalisation pace, validating the rate-convergence bearish EUR/JPY thesis.
signposts.source: S&P Global eurozone services PMI (monthly flash)

signposts.indicator: CFTC JPY Leveraged Funds Net Position
signposts.confirms_if: Stable within 40,000-80,000 range (durable yen-bullish positioning without overcrowding)
signposts.invalidates_if: Surges above +100,000 contracts (overcrowded, squeeze risk) OR reverts to net short below 0 (thesis lost)
signposts.bilateral_importance: The speculative positioning signal for the JPY leg of EUR/JPY; the current moderate-long positioning (75th percentile) provides directional support for yen appreciation without the squeeze risk of an overcrowded position. Monitoring this prevents being caught in a positioning unwind.
signposts.source: CFTC weekly Traders in Financial Futures (TFF) report

signposts.confirms_if: Spot breaks below 182.66 and sustains (trend shift; attracts trend-following selling)
signposts.invalidates_if: Spot rebounds above 50d SMA (185.32) and sustains (uptrend resumes; momentum turns bullish)
signposts.bilateral_importance: Structural trend indicator for the pair itself; a break below 200d SMA would attract systematic trend-following selling and signal that the 13-month uptrend from 155.59 has definitively broken. This level is watched by CTAs and momentum-based funds globally and would trigger additional EUR/JPY selling flows if breached.

assumptions: Carry calculation: Used the ECB deposit rate (2.00%) minus BOJ policy rate (0.75%) for the +125bp differential. Actual all-in carry via FX swaps may differ from the policy-rate differential due to cross-currency basis effects, which were not directly observable for EUR/JPY.
Forward points: Estimated using the interest rate parity approximation from the +125bp differential. Precise EUR/JPY forward quotes were not retrieved from live dealer screens.

Implied volatility: EUR/JPY-specific 1M IV was estimated by combining EUR/USD IV (~10%) and USD/JPY IV (~9.5%) with correlation adjustment. Direct EUR/JPY vol surface data was not publicly available and the estimate carries model risk.

Risk reversals: Inferred from USD/JPY risk reversal direction (JPY-call premium post-intervention) and EUR/USD risk reversal direction (call bid). Direct EUR/JPY risk reversal levels were not sourced.
PPP fair value: Used EUR dossier's EUR/JPY PPP of ~138 and JPY dossier's EUR/JPY PPP of ~110. The ~33% overvaluation is approximate and depends on the PPP methodology (OECD vs IMF vs BigMac).

Bank consensus aggregation: Used ExchangeRates.org.uk bank-aggregated forecasts and LongForecast algorithmic projections as the primary consensus benchmarks. Specific named investment bank EUR/JPY forecasts were not individually retrieved.

Weight adjustments: Risk Appetite boosted by +0.03 at tactical (from 0.10 to 0.13) and +0.03 at strategic (from 0.05 to 0.08) for the pair's moderate equity correlation; USD Liquidity reduced by -0.03 at tactical (from 0.11 to 0.08) and -0.03 at strategic (from 0.12 to 0.09) for this non-USD cross. Net weight sum preserved at 1.00 for both horizons.
Price data integrity: All spot, performance, moving average, and range data derived exclusively from the provided Twelve Data daily OHLC. No web-sourced prices were used for these metrics.
Valuation date: Analysis reflects conditions as of 9 May 2026 (spot close date), not 6 May 2026 (nominal report date). The spot of 184.65 is the 9 May 2026 close.

BOJ June hike probability: Internal estimate of ~45-50% probability is higher than market-implied ~35-40%, reflecting a judgment that three dissenters at the April meeting creates more pressure than markets are pricing. This is an interpretive choice that affects Factor 1 and Factor 6 assessments.
